text:
There once was a charming little town nestled between two great mountains. It was known for its orchards, cozy fox Huntsmen lodges, and, most importantly, its annual apple festival. Everyone loved apples — they ate them, wore them, sang about them. One particular year, the apples were particularly magnificent, and rumors flew that they had miraculous properties.

On festival day, a stranger arrived in town. Tall, mysterious, he wandered into the orchard at dusk. He didn’t speak. Yet everyone could sense an aura of wisdom about him.

As twilight deepened, he plucked an apple from a branch. Everyone watched. It seemed to sparkle. He popped it into his mouth with a silent chew. Then he looked up at them.

“Here,” he whispered solemnly. “Eat.”

And one by one, townsfolk took an apple. They felt a strange buzz inside their heads — perhaps love, maybe just an odd sensation. That night when they slept, they dreamed of
